title: calcearia boridiensis
side_effects: Calcearia boridiensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and swelling in some individuals. In severe cases, prolonged use or excessive consumption can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, the plant may interact with certain medications, causing blood thinning issues, and it may also exacerbate existing conditions, such as kidney or liver problems. Improper use of Calcearia boridiensis can also cause respiratory issues, including difficulty breathing and asthma-like symptoms in sensitive individuals.
